# Model register and status {#sec-catalogue}

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8). Scaffold — the factual columns should be code-generated (see the note at the end); the status column is editorial.
:::

The single answer to "how many models, and which estimate do I trust." Every model in
the suite is listed with its family, outcome, causal status, and role, linking to its
companion page at `docs/models/{id}/index.qmd`.

## Status taxonomy {#sec-register}

- **Model of record (causal)** — the authoritative model for a randomised question; the
  only models the headline **causal** claims draw numbers from.
- **Joint** — the cross-outcome model used for consistency and contrasts.
- **Robustness** — checks on whether a model-of-record effect survives an alternative
  sample, adjustment, or specification (never a new effect).
- **Association (secondary)** — adjusted-association models (mechanism, mediation,
  factor, per-protocol); **no coefficient is causal** beyond the single randomised term,
  if any (@sec-dag-traps).
- **Companion** — a no-interaction or treated-only model that exists only to support a
  comparison (e.g. a nested PSIS-LOO baseline).
- **Superseded** — replaced by a later model and retained only for provenance.
- **Reserved / deferred** — specified but not built; recorded for the roadmap.

## The register

One row per fitted model, generated from `statistical_models/definitions.py` so it
cannot drift from the code. Outcomes marked *(floored)* take the floor rule
(@sec-methods-floor); see @sec-itt, @sec-robustness, @sec-mechanism, @sec-mediation
and @sec-alternative-views for where each family is reported.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-model-register
#| tbl-cap: "The model register — every fitted model, its family, outcome, and status. Generated from statistical_models/definitions.py."
#| echo: false
try:
    import pandas as pd
    from language_reading_predictors.statistical_models.definitions import (
        MODEL_REGISTRY,
        Status,
    )

    _ORDER = {
        s: i
        for i, s in enumerate(
            [
                Status.MODEL_OF_RECORD,
                Status.JOINT,
                Status.ROBUSTNESS,
                Status.ASSOCIATION,
                Status.COMPANION,
            ]
        )
    }
    _rows = [
        {
            "Model": d.model_id.upper(),
            "Family": d.family,
            "Kind": d.kind,
            "Outcome": (d.outcome_label or "—") + (" (floored)" if d.floored else ""),
            "Status": d.status.value,
            "Role": d.role,
        }
        for d in sorted(
            MODEL_REGISTRY.values(),
            key=lambda d: (_ORDER.get(d.status, 99), d.family, d.model_id),
        )
    ]
    _register = pd.DataFrame(_rows).set_index("Model")
except Exception as _exc:  # pragma: no cover - environment dependent
    from IPython.display import Markdown

    _register = Markdown(
        "> **Register pending** — generated from "
        "`language_reading_predictors.statistical_models.definitions`; renders once "
        f"that module is importable in this environment (`{type(_exc).__name__}`)."
    )
_register
```

## Supersession map

The current suite replaced an earlier generation (now deleted; recorded here for
provenance):

- **LRPITT01–11** supersede LRP52 (W), LRP53 (R), LRP54 (E), LRP74 (TE), LRP75 (TR) —
  which conditioned on cross-baselines the DAG shows are unnecessary.
- **LRPITT12** supersedes LRP55 (joint).
- **LRPITT13/14** supersede LRP60/60a (SES).
- **LRPITT15** supersedes LRP76 (generalisation).

## Reserved / deferred

- **LRP16** — a descriptive developmental-trajectory model, deferred (the trajectory
  question is the subject of the companion vocabulary-growth report).
- **LRP70** — a CELF-moderated mechanism, deferred pending a DAG review of conditioning
  on a descendant of letter-sound knowledge.

::: {.callout-note title="Generated, not hand-maintained"}
The register table is generated from `statistical_models/definitions.py` — a pure-data
module (model id, kind, outcome, family, status; no heavy imports) — so it cannot drift
from the code; `tests/test_model_definitions.py` guards the registry against the model
modules and their `SPEC`s. The supersession and reserved lists above mirror that module's
`SUPERSEDED` / `RESERVED` constants. See `notes/202606281200-dse-report-house-style.md`.
:::
